AI_Reason: Compares SVM/RDF/MLP with radiomics features vs CNN-based architectures (ConvNeXt, ResNet) with transfer learning on prostate MRI; separate paradigm AUCs and pretrained foundation model scale not specified.

Title: Exploring the efficacy of multi-flavored feature extraction with radiomics and deep features for prostate cancer grading on mpMRI.
AI_Reason: Compares five feature sets (radiomics, deep, combined) using PyRadiomics + autoencoder on PROSTATEx-2 prostate cancer cohort, but autoencoder pretrained only on 111-patient dataset (well below 100k threshold).

Title: Histogram of Oriented Gradients meet deep learning: A novel multi-task deep network for 2D surgical image semantic segmentation.
AI_Reason: Multi-task deep network for surgical image semantic segmentation using HOG as auxiliary pseudo-labels; not a clinical prediction study comparing FM embeddings vs handcrafted radiomics.

Title: Prognostic Value of a Combined Nomogram Model Integrating 3-Dimensional Deep Learning and Radiomics for Head and Neck Cancer.
AI_Reason: Reports separate TumorStage (0.604/0.571), radiomics (0.851/0.849), 3D deep (0.840/0.832), and combined (0.895/0.900) arms on same HNC PET/CT cohort, but 3D deep model pretraining scale and foundation model identity not specified.

Title: Predicting Osteoporosis and Osteopenia by Fusing Deep Transfer Learning Features and Classical Radiomics Features Based on Single-Source Dual-energy CT Imaging.
AI_Reason: Reports separate radiomics, DTL, and features fusion AUCs on same dual-energy CT lumbar vertebrae cohort across two-level classification, but pretrained foundation model identity not specified.

Title: A fusion model integrating magnetic resonance imaging radiomics and deep learning features for predicting alpha-thalassemia X-linked intellectual disability mutation status in isocitrate dehydrogenase-mutant high-grade astrocytoma: a multicenter study.
AI_Reason: Optimal imaging model fuses radiomics + DL features in same overall lesion region; abstract reports only fused imaging model vs clinical vs fusion-with-clinical AUCs, separate pure-paradigm arms unclear.

Title: A multidomain fusion model of radiomics and deep learning to discriminate between PDAC and AIP based on (18)F-FDG PET/CT images.
AI_Reason: Reports separate RAD_model, DL_model, and MF_model on same PDAC vs AIP PET/CT cohort with multidomain fusion achieving best AUC 0.964, but pretrained foundation model identity not specified.

Title: [Deep transfer learning radiomics model based on temporal bone CT for assisting in the diagnosis of inner ear malformations].
AI_Reason: Reports separate radiomics (AUC 0.929), deep learning (0.947), and DLR fusion (0.964) arms on same temporal bone CT cohort, but pretrained foundation model identity not specified.

Title: Multi-Layer Perceptron Classifier with the Proposed Combined Feature Vector of 3D CNN Features and Lung Radiomics Features for COPD Stage Classification.
AI_Reason: Reports separate 3D CNN features and PyRadiomics lung radiomics and combined MLP classifier AUCs on same COPD HRCT cohort; 3D CNN uses transfer learning but pretraining scale and foundation model identity not specified.

Title: End-to-end prediction of clinical outcomes in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma with foundation model-based multiple instance learning.
Human_Reason: Peer-reviewed version of preprint PMID 39974018. FM-based MIL vs handcrafted radiomics head-to-head on 2485-patient HNSCC CT cohort with separate per-arm AUROCs (FM 0.75-0.84 vs radiomics 0.64-0.74). All four inclusion criteria pass.
Notes: Identified via cross-reference from preprint PMID 39974018 (PRISMA "other methods" branch). Published BMC Artif Intell 2025;1(1):3, DOI 10.1186/s44398-025-00003-8.

Title: Noninvasive preoperative risk stratification of prostate cancer via a foundational model based deep learning with PSMA PET/CT.
AI_Reason: HIGH-confidence Claude.ai pre-screen: BiomedCLIP and MedSAM (named FMs) head-to-head against conventional radiomics on PSMA PET/CT prostate cancer cohort.
Human_Reason: Confirmed: BiomedCLIP-only (AUC 0.764) AND MedSAM (AUC 0.759) head-to-head vs conventional radiomics/RF (AUC 0.676) on same 494-patient PSMA PET/CT cohort with 5-fold CV. Both BiomedCLIP and MedSAM are named FMs in our protocol.
Notes: Was in batch_19 of AI screening but row absent from CSV (data-integrity gap). Published BMC Cancer 2026, DOI 10.1186/s12885-026-15715-x.
